title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Miss Blacklock informs the inspector that Randall Goddard's will names the descendants of Sonia as heirs. She specifies that Sonia has two legitimate children, twins named Pip and Emma, who are approximately twenty-six years old. Based on this information, the inspector concludes that these siblings have a motive to kill Miss Blacklock and warns her that another murder attempt may occur.

In a separate encounter, Inspector Corradino questions Philippa Hammersley—also referred to as Mrs. Havers, Mrs. Himmelstoss, or Mrs. Hemes—while she is weeding a flower bed. Corradino accuses her of meeting Rudolf Scherz at a gazebo to discuss a hiding place at 6:15, the time a robbery occurred. Philippa denies ever seeing Scherz and claims she was working in the garden all morning. Corradino notes a hint of fear in her voice, while his colleague Fletcher suggests she is lying due to a grudge held by a girl named Micky. Corradino remains uncertain whether to believe the foreign girl or Mrs. Hammersley.

Later, Corradino sits in a garden with Miss Marple. Sensing impending danger, Corradino warns Miss Marple that it is unsafe to snoop. Miss Marple defends her tendency to pry, explaining that asking questions helps determine if people are truly who they claim to be. She observes that since the war, village life has changed; unlike her home of St. Mary Mead, modern villages are filled with strangers from all over the world who settle in without proof of their identities or character.
